title: ds-viz-events - option matrix
description: Test bench for splunk.events. The 10.4 reference exposes 6 options: backgroundColor, eventActions, fieldActions, footerFields, highlightValuesByField, showFieldSummary. Pairs with a secondary `fieldsummary` data source via dataSources.fieldsummary when showFieldSummary is true.

options.query: | makeresults count=8 | streamstats count AS rn | eval host = case(rn%4==0,"web-01", rn%4==1,"web-02", rn%4==2,"db-01", 1==1,"app-03") | eval source = case(rn<=2,"/var/log/access.log", rn<=5,"/var/log/app.log", 1==1,"/var/log/error.log") | eval sourcetype = case(rn<=2,"access_combined", rn<=5,"app_json", 1==1,"error_log") | eval status = case(rn==1,200, rn==2,404, rn==3,500, rn==4,200, rn==5,201, rn==6,503, rn==7,500, 1==1,200) | eval _raw = "" . strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") . " host=" . host . " status=" . tostring(status) . " message=request_completed" | table _time _raw host source sourcetype status

| makeresults count=3 | streamstats count AS rn | eval host = case(rn==1,"web-01", rn==2,"web-02", 1==1,"db-01") | eval _raw = "" . strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") . " host=" . host . " status=200" | table _time _raw host

options.markdown:
# `splunk.events` - option matrix

The events viewer renders raw `_raw` log payloads with expandable per-event metadata. Use it instead of `splunk.table` when:

- The user needs the **raw event text** (`_raw`) preserved with field decorations.
- The dashboard supports **field actions** (drilldowns from individual field values) or **event actions** (from the whole row).
- The data has highly variable fields per event - the events viewer adapts; tables don't.

6 options total. Pairs with an optional secondary data source (`dataSources.fieldsummary`) for the field summary panel.

viz_default.title: 1. Default - showFieldSummary=true with fieldsummary data source
viz_default.description: The default events viewer. dataSources.primary holds the events; dataSources.fieldsummary holds a `| fieldsummary maxvals=10` chain off the primary. The left rail shows fields and value distributions.

viz_no_summary.title: 2. showFieldSummary: false (no left rail)
viz_no_summary.description: Strips the field summary rail. The viewer becomes a pure event list. Use this when you don't have the screen real estate for the rail or when the events have stable schemas where the rail adds no signal.

viz_bg_color.title: 3. backgroundColor: panel tint
viz_bg_color.description: Custom backgroundColor for the events panel. Different from the panel chrome - tints the events area itself.

viz_footer.title: 4. footerFields: ["host", "source"]
viz_footer.description: Pin specific fields into the per-event footer. The viewer normally requires expansion to read field values; footerFields surfaces the most important ones inline.

viz_highlight.title: 5. highlightValuesByField
viz_highlight.description: Highlight specific field-value pairs across all events. Useful for surfacing investigation pivots (the IP under review, the affected host).

viz_compact.title: 6. Compact - 3 events, no summary, no footer
viz_compact.description: A small events viewer for sidebar context. With only 3 events and no field summary, this fits in tight grid cells next to a primary chart.

viz_event_actions.title: 7. eventActions - row-level click handler
viz_event_actions.description: Define an action that fires when the user clicks an event row. The label appears in a row-level menu. Pair with primary.click drilldowns to drive deeper investigation.

viz_field_actions.title: 8. fieldActions - per-field click handler
viz_field_actions.description: Define actions that appear in field-level dropdowns. Use the `default` key for actions that should appear for ALL fields, or key by specific field name. Event types should end in `.click` if you wire event handlers.

viz_full.title: 9. Full kitchen-sink - all options stacked
viz_full.description: Field summary on, custom background, footer fields pinned, highlights active, and both event + field actions wired up. The viewer carries every documented capability simultaneously.
